description: Single config DocType for the Wave <> Hypa integration. All rules live here so admins can edit them without a deploy.

fields.description: Master kill-switch for the whole integration. When off, inbound webhooks are still received, authenticated and logged, then acknowledged with HTTP 200 (so Wave does not retry) with the payload kept for replay - but nothing is enqueued, no outbound call is made to Wave, and no ERP record is changed as a result.
fields.label: Integration Enabled

fields.description: System User the inbound Wave processing runs as, so webhook-created records are attributed to it instead of Guest. Leave blank to keep running as Guest. The user must be able to read Warehouse (e.g. Stock User/Manager) for Pick List reconciliation.
fields.label: Wave Integration User

fields.description: When on, the intake matches Wave customers to existing ERP customers by email when the Wave ID lookup misses — preventing duplicates for customers added manually or imported. Skips ambiguous matches (same email on multiple ERP customers, or an email already linked to a different Wave ID) and logs a warning. Default off.
fields.label: Customer Email Fallback Lookup Enabled

fields.description: When on, Wave's COLLECTED webhook reconciles the matching Pick List and auto-submits it if still in Draft. Already-submitted or cancelled Pick Lists only get a Comment summarising Wave's reported pick — never modified. Default off.
fields.label: Pick List Inbound Submit Enabled

fields.description: Tax template applied to the shipping fee line at intake. When set, the line rate is back-calculated so rate + tax equals Wave's reported shipping amount exactly. Leave blank to use Wave's amount as the rate directly and let the SO's master tax template apply on top.
fields.label: Shipping Item Tax Template

fields.description: When on, SOs flagged for manual review also create a Frappe ToDo so the team is pinged in 'My Tasks'. Recipient is the Assignee field below, falling back to all users in the Role. Default off.
fields.label: Intake Review ToDo Enabled

fields.description: Single User to receive intake-review ToDos. Wins over the Role field below when set. Leave blank to fan out to every user in the configured Role instead.
fields.label: Intake Review Assignee

fields.description: Role whose members receive intake-review ToDos when no single Assignee is set. One ToDo per user with this role.
fields.label: Intake Review Role

fields.description: Divisor applied to every Wave amount to convert to major currency units. Wave sends amounts in minor units (cents); leave as 100 unless that changes.
fields.label: Price Scale Divisor

fields.description: Wave Sync Log rows older than this many days are deleted by the daily retention task.
fields.label: Log Retention (days)

fields.description: Shared secret sent by Wave in the x-api-key header on every webhook. Must be exactly 32 URL-safe characters (A-Z, a-z, 0-9, underscore, hyphen). Stored encrypted.
fields.label: Inbound API Key (32 URL-safe chars)

fields.description: Base URL for the Wave REST API, e.g. https://dev.hypaafrica.api.wavegrocery.com
fields.label: Wave API Base URL

fields.description: Sent as the appId HTTP header on every outbound call. Provided by Wave when the app is registered.
fields.label: Wave App ID

fields.description: Identifier of the Wave store this ERP instance mirrors. Single-shop deployments leave this as 1.
fields.label: Wave Store ID

fields.description: Wave's internal mongo _id of the shop, used as `shopId` when pushing ERP-originated orders to Wave's create-order endpoint. Distinct from Wave Store ID above — that's the human-readable storeID ("1"); this is the longer hex _id (e.g. 698ef51f728782a10adcef6d). Get it from any Wave order payload's shop._id or from Wave admin.
fields.label: Wave Shop ID (mongo _id)

fields.description: API key sent on every outbound call to Wave (X-API-Key header). Stored encrypted.
fields.label: Wave API Key

fields.description: Master kill-switch for outbound stock pushes. When off, ERP stock movements are still logged but no HTTP call is made to Wave. Use this to mute traffic during cutovers without disabling the inbound integration.
fields.label: Outbound Stock Sync Enabled

fields.description: When on, every stock-sync push is followed by a PATCH that writes quantityLimit = same quantity, capping how much customers can order to what's actually in stock. Fires only after stock_sync_enabled succeeds; a failure here logs a Warning but never reverts the stock push. Default off.
fields.label: Outbound Stock Caps Max Quantity Enabled

fields.description: Master kill-switch for outbound order-status pushes. When off, Sales Order submit/cancel events are observed and logged but no PUT is made to Wave. Independent from the stock kill-switch so ops can mute one channel at a time.
fields.label: Outbound Order Status Sync Enabled

fields.description: When on, every Pick List creation pushes its items + identifiers (batch numbers, SKU, or barcode — per Picker Identifier Source below) to Wave's picker app. Independent of the ACCEPTED status push, which is gated by Outbound Status Rules. Default off.
fields.label: Pick List Batch IDs Push Enabled

fields.description: What identifier the picker app scans per Pick List line. Blank sends ERPNext batch numbers (one per allocated batch row); 'Item Code' sends the SKU; 'Item Barcode' sends the Item's first Barcode row. Inbound reconciliation expects Wave to echo back the same identifier — mismatches block auto-submit for operator review.
fields.label: Picker Identifier Source

fields.description: When on, ERP-side Pick List submit and cancel require the 'Pick List Wave Override' role (System Manager always passes). Use this once Wave's picker app is the source of truth — direct ERP submissions become a manager-only escape hatch. Default off.
fields.label: Pick List ERP Submit Lockdown Enabled

fields.description: Driver auto-stamped on Delivery Notes whose linked Wave SO is a Pickup order. Leave blank to skip — operators then pick the driver manually. The auto-stamp only applies on DN creation when the driver field is empty; later operator edits are never overwritten.
fields.label: Wave Pickup Driver

fields.description: When on, operators can push offline-created Sales Orders to Wave via the 'Push to Wave' button on the SO form. Wave creates the order in its catalog so the picker app picks up the workflow. Default off.
fields.label: ERP → Wave Order Push Enabled

fields.description: Wave mongo _id of the fallback customer used when the Sales Order's linked ERP Customer has no wave_customer_id stamped. Pushes fail with a notification if this is blank AND the linked Customer has no cached Wave id.
fields.label: Common Offline Customer (Wave _id)

fields.description: paymentType sent on every ERP-pushed order. Must match one of Wave's enum values (cash, card, klarna, mobile, thirdPartyReference, cardOnDelivery, bankTransfer, irisOnDelivery).
fields.label: Default Offline Payment Type

fields.description: When on, a failed ERP → Wave push also creates a Frappe ToDo for the configured Intake Review Assignee / Role so the issue is escalated to operators. Comment + form banner are always added; only the ToDo is gated by this. Default on.
fields.label: Push Failure ToDo Enabled

fields.description: Company applied to every Sales Order created by this integration.
fields.label: Default Company

fields.description: Warehouse used as set_warehouse on every Sales Order.
fields.label: Default Warehouse

fields.description: Selling Price List that drives line-item rates. ERP is the pricing source of truth; Wave's prices are ignored.
fields.label: Default Price List

fields.description: Currency for every Sales Order created by this integration.
fields.label: Default Currency

fields.description: Placeholder Item used as the fallback line when none of an order's Wave SKUs resolve to ERP Items. Leave blank to abort such orders with a loud Error + ToDo instead. When set, the SO is flagged for manual review so operators re-key the real items.
fields.label: Default Unresolved Items Placeholder

fields.description: Customer Group assigned to Customers newly created from Wave webhooks.
fields.label: Default Customer Group

fields.description: Territory assigned to Customers newly created from Wave webhooks.
fields.label: Default Territory

fields.description: Fallback Customer for guest orders (payload isGuest=true). Must exist in ERP before orders are processed.
fields.label: Walk-in Customer

fields.description: Credit limit set (for the Default Company) on Customers newly created from Wave. Applied once at creation only; later changes are left to operators.
fields.label: Default Customer Credit Limit

fields.description: All rule tables are editable here. The integration reads them at dispatch time, so changes take effect immediately after save.
fields.label: Rules

fields.description: Routes (Wave doc_type, action) pairs to a registered handler. Disabled rows are skipped.
fields.label: Route Rules

fields.description: Inbound status transitions. Each row tells the order update handler what to do when Wave reports the given status.
fields.label: Inbound Status Rules

fields.description: Outbound status pushes. Each row maps an ERP lifecycle event to the status string sent to Wave's changeorderstatus endpoint.
fields.label: Outbound Status Rules

fields.description: Wave fees (SHIPPING_COST, PLASTIC_BAGS, ...) are added as Sales Order lines using the ERP Item configured here.
fields.label: Fee Mappings

fields.description: Sales Taxes and Charges Templates applied to Sales Orders created from Wave webhooks. The first enabled, existing, non-disabled row wins.
fields.label: Tax Rules

fields.description: Maps each Wave paymentType to a prepaid/cod classification and an ERP Mode of Payment. The intake stamps the classification on the Sales Order; the Payment Entry validator uses it to confirm submitted PEs match Wave's expected payment class.
fields.label: Payment Method Mappings

fields.description: Maps a Wave businessType (e.g. restaurant/cafe/hotel) to an ERP Customer Group for B2B customers. Matched fuzzily: a row matches when its key is contained in the incoming businessType (or vice-versa); the longest matching key wins. No match falls back to Default Customer Group.
fields.label: Business Type Mappings

fields.description: When on, any inbound Wave order carrying a coupon raises a review alarm (ToDo + Sales Order review flag); an existing matching ERP coupon is applied to the order, and a coupon not present in ERP is flagged only. ERP coupons are never created or updated from Wave. Off => coupons in the payload are ignored.
fields.label: Coupon Review Alarm Enabled

fields.description: When on, prepaid orders are verified against the same-site iPay app: a payment lookup fires on order creation and via the 'Verify iPay Payment' button on the Sales Order. Off => no iPay lookups (the integration still works; payments just aren't auto-verified).
fields.label: iPay Verification Enabled

fields.description: When on, wave_sync_hypa creates each prepaid order's Payment Entry itself: an unallocated draft once the iPay payment is confirmed on a submitted Sales Order, then attached to the Sales Invoice and submitted. Off => the external automation (n8n) stays the sole creator. Flip on only in lock-step with disabling the n8n Payment Entry node.
fields.label: iPay Auto-Create Payment Entry

fields.description: User who receives a ToDo when a prepaid order's iPay payment cannot be verified (iPay absent, not paid, or unreachable). Leave blank to rely on the form banner + the 'Payment Review Required' list filter alone.
fields.label: Payment Review Assignee

name: Wave Settings
